person.name: Joan Mowbray
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Evidence of Joan Mowbray's maiden name is found on the monumental inscription of her son, Henry Gray of Ketteringham, Norfolk, which reads as follows:
"Here lyeth Syr Henry Grey, the sonne of Syre Thomas Grey, and Jone, hys wyf, that was systere to the Duke of Norfolk, that dyed at Venis." [Reference: Scott, Memorials of the Family of Scott (1876), p. 126 footnote p (reference courtesy of Dr. David Faris); see also Norfolk Archaeology, 3 (1852): 274-275.].

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Mowbray於1364年3月25日出生在英國林可爾史爾艾普沃斯時，她的父親Sir John de Mowbray III23歲，母親Elizabeth Segrave25歲。她於大約1382年與Sir Thomas Gray Knt.結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1407年6月8日之後在英國藍卡爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Mowbray于1364年3月25日出生在英国林可爾史爾艾普沃斯时，她的父亲Sir John de Mowbray III23岁，母亲Elizabeth Segrave25岁。她于大约1382年与Sir Thomas Gray Knt.结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1407年6月8日之后在英国藍卡爾。

nameMeanings.name: Mowbray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from Montbray (Manche). Robert de Mowbray was Earl of Northumbria 1086–95, but was later imprisoned and deposed for rebelling against William II.

variants.name: Mulberry
meanings: English (Middlesex and Cambridgeshire): variant of   Mowbray  .
Irish (Derry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilbhearaigh ‘descendant of the devotee of (Saint) Bearach’.

variants.name: Mayberry
meanings: English: most probably an altered form of   Mowbray  . It is also found as   Maybury  , which has the form of an English habitational name. There is a place near Woking in Surrey so called; however, this is not recorded until 1885 and is probably derived from the surname. In England this surname is found mainly in the West Midlands; it has also spread into Wales. In Ireland this form is common in Ulster; MacLysaght records that it was taken there from England in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Mowbray was born on 25 March 1364, in Epworth, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Sir John de Mowbray III, was 23 and her mother, Elizabeth Segrave, was 25. She married Sir Thomas Gray Knt. about 1382. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 8 June 1407, in Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Mowbray est née le 25 mars 1364, à Epworth, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Mowbray III, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Segrave, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Gray Knt. environ 1382. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 8 juin 1407, à Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Mowbray am 25. März 1364 in Epworth, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Mowbray III 23 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Segrave 25. Sie hat um 1382 Sir Thomas Gray Knt. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 8. Juni 1407 in Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Mowbray è nata il il 25 marzo 1364 nel Epworth, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Mowbray III, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Segrave, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Gray Knt. circa 1382. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 8 giugno 1407, nel Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Mowbrayが1364年3月25日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアエプウオルスで生まれた時，父Sir John de Mowbray IIIは23歳，母Elizabeth Segraveは25歳であった。約1382年にSir Thomas Gray Knt.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1407年6月8日以後にイギリスランカシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Mowbray(이)가 1364년 3월 25일 영국 링코실르 p워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Mowbray III(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Segrave(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1382년 Sir Thomas Gray Knt.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1407년 6월 8일 이후 영국 랭카실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Mowbray родилась 25 марта 1364 г. в местности эпворт, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Mowbray III было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Segrave, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Gray Knt. около 1382 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 8 июнь 1407 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия.
